facts.statement: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. describes itself as an automated global broker that custodies and services accounts for hedge funds, mutual funds, ETFs, registered investment advisors, proprietary trading groups, introducing brokers, and individual investors.

The company says it specializes in routing orders and executing and processing trades in stocks, options, futures, foreign exchange instruments, bonds, mutual funds, ETFs, precious metals, and forecast contracts.
Customers can use the company's trading platform to trade certain cryptocurrencies through third-party cryptocurrency service providers that execute, clear, and custody the cryptocurrencies.

Management says the company has focused since inception on developing proprietary software to automate broker-dealer functions and has used automation to integrate with an increasing number of trading venues.

The company states that nearly five decades of developing automated trading platforms and automating middle- and back-office functions have helped it become one of the lowest-cost providers of broker-dealer services.
As of December 31, 2025, Interactive Brokers said it had 3,182 employees worldwide.

The company says it operates from Greenwich, Connecticut and Chicago in the U.S., and abroad through offices in Canada, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Switzerland, Hungary, Dubai, India, China (Hong Kong and Shanghai), Japan, Singapore, and Australia.
SEC company facts show revenue of $1.557 billion for FY2025.
SEC company facts show operating cash flow of $4.544 billion for FY2025.
SEC company facts show cash and equivalents of $3.753 billion and stockholders' equity of $4.28 billion for FY2025.
IBG, Inc. says its primary asset is ownership of approximately 26.3% of the membership interests of IBG LLC, and that it is the sole managing member of IBG LLC.

The company says the remaining approximately 73.7% of IBG LLC membership interests are held by IBG Holdings LLC, which is owned directly and indirectly by founder and Chairman Thomas Peterffy and affiliates, management and other employees, and certain other members.
Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. says its common stock trades on Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol IBKR.
The company states it joined the S&P 500 Index on August 28, 2025.

The company says its roots trace to a market making business founded by Chairman Thomas Peterffy on the floor of the American Stock Exchange in 1977, and that Interactive Brokers was formed in 1993 after his earlier automated market-making work.
On April 15, 2025, the company announced a four-for-one forward stock split in the form of a stock dividend, and said each holder of record on June 16, 2025 received three additional shares.
As of February 23, 2026, the company reported 445,439,458 Class A shares outstanding and 400 Class B shares outstanding.

The proxy statement says all outstanding Class B shares are held by IBG Holdings LLC, and that Class B shares are voted on an as-converted basis so they do not provide supervoting rights; the company says Holdings is expected to control approximately 73.7% of votes eligible at the 2026 annual meeting.
The 10-K identifies Milan Galik as Chief Executive Officer and President, and Paul J. Brody as Chief Financial Officer, Treasurer, and Secretary.
Thomas Peterffy is identified as founder and Chairman; the 10-K says he served as Chief Executive Officer from November 2006 to September 2019.

The company says its Code of Ethics and Business Conduct applies to all directors, officers, and employees, including the CEO, CFO, and Chief Accounting Officer, and that it has adopted an Insider Trading Policy covering directors, officers, employees, and certain family members and controlled entities.

Management concluded that disclosure controls and procedures and internal control over financial reporting were effective as of December 31, 2025, and Deloitte & Touche LLP issued an unqualified opinion on internal control over financial reporting.

The proxy statement says the 2007 Stock Incentive Plan had 35,930,685 restricted stock units available for future issuance as of December 31, 2025, and that the company granted 1,876,533 RSU awards during 2025, including 317,620 to executive officers.

The board adopted resolutions on February 26, 2026, subject to stockholder approval, to amend the 2007 Stock Incentive Plan to extend its term through April 24, 2037; the plan permits awards to directors, officers, contractors, and consultants and has been used only for RSUs to date.
